description: One-lever discipline guard for FrankenLibC optimization waves. Each optimization bead must reference exactly one lever category and carry replayable golden-output, rollback, and attribution metadata so every wave stays attributable, reversible, and behavior-proofed.

lever_categories.description: Taxonomy of optimization levers. Each optimization opportunity must be classified under exactly one category.

stub_elimination.description: Replace Stub symbols with working implementations. Transitions status from Stub to Implemented.

callthrough_removal.description: Replace GlibcCallThrough symbols with native Rust or raw syscall implementations.

simd_acceleration.description: Add SIMD (SSE/AVX2/AVX-512/NEON) fast paths for compute-intensive operations.

cache_optimization.description: Improve cache behavior: layout, prefetch, TLS caching, lookup tables.

dispatch_optimization.description: Improve function dispatch: branch prediction, indirect call elimination, inlining.

allocation_optimization.description: Improve memory allocation performance: arena sizing, slab allocation, free-list tuning.

syscall_optimization.description: Reduce syscall overhead: batching, vDSO, io_uring, syscall elimination.

subsystem_implementation.description: Implement a new subsystem required for replacement level transition. High effort, architectural scope.

entry_contract.description: Every optimization-wave opportunity must carry enough metadata to replay semantics, attribute the hotspot, and roll back the lever in isolation.

enforcement.description: Rules for lever assignment and guard enforcement.
rules: Every opportunity_matrix entry must have a lever_category field
The lever_category value must be one of the defined categories
Each optimization bead should address exactly one lever category
Multi-lever beads must be split or carry a justification_waiver
Every opportunity must declare replayable golden-output verification metadata
Every opportunity must declare a single-lever rollback plan and attribution metadata
